description: LLM-as-judge scoring rubric for Tryps launch video copy. Four judge personas score independently on 5 engagement dimensions. Composite = weighted average across personas and dimensions.

dimensions.name: scroll_stop
dimensions.description: Would this make someone stop scrolling in their feed? Does the opening line create an instant pattern interrupt? Punchy > polished. Specific > generic. Unexpected > familiar.

scoring.0: Completely invisible — would scroll past without registering
scoring.3: Mildly interesting but doesn't demand attention
scoring.5: Decent hook but feels like an ad, not a moment
scoring.7: Strong — would pause for a second, maybe read again
scoring.9: Excellent — thumb stops, attention locked, need to know more
scoring.10: Perfect pattern interrupt — impossible to scroll past

dimensions.name: share_impulse
dimensions.description: Would someone tag a friend, send this to a group chat, or screenshot it? The ultimate test: does this make the reader think of a specific person to share it with?

scoring.0: No impulse to share — feels like a billboard, not a conversation starter
scoring.3: Might save it privately but wouldn't send to anyone
scoring.5: Could see sharing if someone asked, but no spontaneous impulse
scoring.7: Would tag one friend or drop in one group chat
scoring.9: Immediately thinks of 2-3 people to send this to
scoring.10: Can't NOT share this — it's too relatable/funny/true to keep to yourself

dimensions.name: identity_resonance
dimensions.description: Does this feel like something the target demo would repost to say 'this is so me' or 'why is this so accurate'? Does it capture a feeling they've had but never articulated?

scoring.0: Generic — could be about anyone, doesn't feel personal
scoring.5: Recognizable situation but described in a generic way
scoring.7: Nails a specific feeling — 'oh they GET it'
scoring.9: Eerily accurate — feels like it was written about their life
scoring.10: Instant identity capture — 'I'm in this video and I don't even care'

dimensions.name: curiosity_gap
dimensions.description: Does this create enough tension or intrigue to make someone watch the full video, click through, or learn more? Is there an open loop that demands closure?

scoring.0: No tension — everything is stated, nothing to discover
scoring.3: Mild curiosity but easy to ignore
scoring.5: Interesting enough to watch if already idle, but not compelling
scoring.7: Clear open loop — need to know what happens next or what this is
scoring.9: Strong tension — feels incomplete without watching, must resolve
scoring.10: Irresistible gap — the brain physically cannot leave this unresolved

dimensions.name: brand_clarity
dimensions.description: Within 3 seconds, do you understand what Tryps is and why it exists? Is the value prop clear without being stated? Does it feel like a real product, not a concept?

scoring.0: No idea what this is or why it exists
scoring.3: Vaguely travel-related but unclear what the product does
scoring.5: Understand it's a travel app but not the group/social angle
scoring.7: Clear: it's a group trip planning tool, and the problem is obvious
scoring.9: Crystal clear value prop AND the brand personality comes through
scoring.10: Instantly get it, want it, and understand how it's different from everything else

composite_score: Weighted average across all dimensions (0-100 scale). Formula: sum(dimension_score * weight * 10). Then averaged across all 4 judge personas with persona weights.
